Package org.rodinp.internal.core

Class CreateInternalElementOperation

java.lang.Object
org.rodinp.internal.core.RodinDBOperation
org.rodinp.internal.core.CreateInternalElementOperation
All Implemented Interfaces:
org.eclipse.core.resources.IWorkspaceRunnable, org.eclipse.core.runtime.ICoreRunnable, org.eclipse.core.runtime.IProgressMonitor
Direct Known Subclasses:
CreateFreshInternalElementOperation
public class CreateInternalElementOperation extends RodinDBOperation

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    IRodinDBStatus
    verify()
    Possible failures: NO_ELEMENTS_TO_PROCESS - the newElement supplied to the operation is null. READ_ONLY - the parent of the newElement supplied is readonly. NAME_COLLISION - the newElement supplied already exists and creating it anew would create a duplicate element. INVALID_SIBLING - the sibling supplied to the operation has a different parent. ELEMENT_DOES_NOT_EXIST - the sibling supplied doesn't exist.

    Methods inherited from class org.rodinp.internal.core.RodinDBOperation

    beginTask, done, executeNestedOperation, getResultElements, getRodinDB, hasModifiedResource, internalWorked, isCanceled, modifiesResources, newRodinElementDelta, run, runOperation, setCanceled, setTaskName, subTask, worked

  • Constructor Details

    • CreateInternalElementOperation

      public CreateInternalElementOperation(InternalElement newElement, IInternalElement nextSibling)

    • CreateInternalElementOperation

      public CreateInternalElementOperation(InternalElement parent, IInternalElement nextSibling, boolean unused)

  • Method Details

    • verify

      public IRodinDBStatus verify()
      Possible failures:
      • NO_ELEMENTS_TO_PROCESS - the newElement supplied to the operation is null.
      • READ_ONLY - the parent of the newElement supplied is readonly.
      • NAME_COLLISION - the newElement supplied already exists and creating it anew would create a duplicate element.
      • INVALID_SIBLING - the sibling supplied to the operation has a different parent.
      • ELEMENT_DOES_NOT_EXIST - the sibling supplied doesn't exist.
